questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The image of an object placed in air formed by a convex refracting surface is at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> behind the surface. The image is real and is at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mi>r</mi><mi>d</mi></mrow></msup><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> of the distance of the object from the surface. The wavelength of light inside the surface is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> times the wavelength in air. The radius of the curved surface is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>13</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is ______ .
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A light ray is incident, at an incident angle θ1, on the system of two plane mirrors M1 and M2 having an inclination angle 75° between them (as shown in figure). After reflecting from mirror M1 it gets reflected back by the mirror M2 with an angle of reflection 30°. The total deviation of the ray will be _____ degree.
questions.solution:
From the above figure, i2=30°,

Now, 

∠OBA+∠ABR+∠RBP=180°⇒2∠OBA+60°=180°⇒∠OBA=60°

In triangle OAB,

∠OBA+∠AOB+∠OAB=180°⇒60°+75°+∠OAB=180°⇒∠OAB=45°

And,

∠OAB+∠BAI+∠IAQ=180°⇒2∠OAB+2i1=180°⇒i1=45°

Now the total deviation of the ray will be,

δ=360°-2i1+i2⇒δ=360°-230°+45°δ=210°
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An unpolarized light beam is incident on the polarizer of a polarization experiment and the intensity of light beam emerging from the analyzer is measured as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn></math> Lumens. Now, if the analyzer is rotated around the horizontal axis (direction of light) by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>°</mo></math> in clockwise direction, the intensity of emerging light will be _______Lumens.
questions.solution:
Assuming initially axis of Polarizer and Analyzer are parallel

Now emerging intensity =I02cos230°

=100322=100×34=75
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
In the given figure, the face AC of the equilateral prism is immersed in a liquid of refractive index n. For incident angle 60° at the side AC, the refracted light beam just grazes along face AC. The refractive index of the liquid n=x4. The value of x is _____ .
(Given refractive index of glass =1.5)
questions.solution:
When the refracted light just grazes the boundary the incident angle is called a critical angle.

Using snell's law at face AC.

1.5sin60°=n×sin90° 

⇒1.5×32=n=x4 

⇒33=x

⇒x=27
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Sodium light of wavelengths <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>650</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>655</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math> is used to study diffraction at a single slit of aperture <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. The distance between the slit and the screen is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. The separation between the positions of the first maxima of diffraction pattern obtained in the two cases is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>
questions.solution:
The condition for maxima in diffraction is,

bsinθ=2n+1λ2.

 For the first maxima n=1. Therefore,

bsinθ=3λ2⇒sinθ=3λ2b=yDy=3λD2b

Now the difference between the positions of the maxima will be,

y1-y2=3D2bλ1-λ2⇒y1-y2=3×22×5×10-4×655-650×10-9=3×10-5 m
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A beam of light consisting of two wavelengths 7000 Ao and 5500 Ao is used to obtain interference pattern in Young's double slit experiment. The distance between the slits is 2.5 mm and the distance between the plane of slits and the screen is 150 cm. The least distance from the central fringe, where the bright fringes due to both the wavelengths coincide, is n×10–5 m. The value of n is _____.
questions.solution:
The given data is

λ1=7000 Ao

λ2=5500 Ao

d=2.5×10-3 m

D=1.5 m

The path difference is given by 

nλ1=mλ2

7n=5.5 m

⇒14n=11 m⇒n=11 and m=14

The formula for the distance of a bright fringe is

y=nλ1Dd

⇒y=11×7×10-7×1.52.5×10-3

=46.2×10-4=462×10-5

It is given that 

n×10-5=462×10-5⇒n=462
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
An object is placed at a distance of 12 cm from a convex lens. A convex mirror of focal length 15 cm is placed on another side of the lens at 8 cm as shown in the figure. The image of the object coincides with the object.

When the convex mirror is removed, a real and inverted image is formed at a position. The distance of the image from the object will be ___ cm
questions.solution: For the object to coincide with the image, the light must fall perpendicularly to the mirror. This means that the light will have to converge at C of the mirror. Without the mirror also, the light would coverage at C. So the distance is:=12+8+30=50 cm.
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
